title: hieracium transiens subsp. phaedrochlorum
side_effects: Hieracium transiens subsp. phaedrochlorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, in individuals who consume it in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to its active compounds may also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory distress. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Hieracium transiens subsp. phaedrochlorum may interfere with blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding. Furthermore, its improper use may also interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
